People: 0715: While laying down from aloft Jril Jackson (Sea) fell overboard from main rigging & saved himself by means of a life line.

Other: 1240: Passed through quantities of yellowish + brownish material, having the appearance of dust, lying in streaks, in and upon the water.

Note: 1645: Anchored. Brgs: Sugar Loaf SSW; Fort Santa Cruz SxE1/2E. -22.91331 -43.14350
1710: Pay Island brg NW, May Id NNE1/4E. cc ESE.

Other: 2340: At 11:40 PM, observed a brilliant meteor passing from east to west over an arc of about 90o.

2230: At 1030 PM observed a very bright meteor, its bearing about NW. It commenced about 30o above the horizon and pursued a westerly course at angle of 20o with the horizon, and disappeared about 5o above the horizon. When first seen, its color was purple, rapidly changing to green and white.

Noon: By order of Comd'g Officer; Those men who are now without white clothes & who should have them, will be considered as on extra duty when the crew are dressed in white or partially in white, and will perform such work as they may be called upon to do during the time the crew remain in whites.

Other: 1100: Between 11 and 11:30 o'clock, the wind moderated considerably, the water changed from a blue to light green in color, and in temperature fell from 73o to 66o with moderate sea; at the same time there was a corresponding fall of wet and dry bulb thermometers.
